key_repos.name: aibtc-mcp-server
key_repos.description: Bitcoin-native MCP server for AI agents (150+ tools)

key_repos.name: x402-api
key_repos.description: x402 payment protocol API endpoints (Cloudflare Workers)

key_repos.name: x402-sponsor-relay
key_repos.description: x402 sponsor relay for gasless Stacks transactions

key_repos.name: btcfi-yield-scanner
key_repos.description: sBTC yield comparison across Zest, ALEX, Bitflow

findings.title: SQL keyword blacklist in StorageDO.ts can be bypassed
findings.detail: String-matching blacklist can be bypassed with nested comments, encoding tricks, or quoting tricks
findings.fix: Replace with whitelist (SELECT only) or proper SQL AST parser (~50 lines)

findings.title: Missing withdraw function — vault is one-way deposit only
findings.detail: Users can deposit sBTC but have no way to withdraw. Vault functions: deposit(amount) but no withdraw(amount)
findings.fix: Add withdraw(amount) function with balance verification and sBTC transfer (~25 lines Clarity)

findings.title: Hardcoded STX/sBTC exchange rates drift from market
findings.detail: Pricing hardcoded to STX=$0.50, sBTC=$100k. Drifts as market moves, causing inaccurate x402 payments
findings.fix: Integrate live price feed (Bitflow oracle or CoinGecko) with KV caching (5 min TTL) (~40 lines)

findings.title: In-memory rate limit map grows unbounded (memory leak)
findings.detail: Rate-limit middleware uses Map that never evicts expired entries. Per-isolate (no cross-instance sharing)
findings.fix: Migrate to KV-backed rate limiting with TTL, or add periodic cleanup (~60 lines)

findings.title: No input size limits on KV and Paste storage writes
findings.detail: Endpoints accept values without size limits. Payer can exhaust DO storage quotas (DoS vector)
findings.fix: Add max size validation (1MB KV, 10MB paste). Return 413 if exceeded (~30 lines)

findings.title: Opportunity: Joint yield scanner MCP tool development
findings.detail: Stark Comet collaboration, direct Zest Protocol alignment. Could add to aibtc-mcp-server as MCP tool
findings.fix: Suggest joint development of yield_scanner_query(token, protocol) tool for MCP server

summary: Jagged Basilisk is the AIBTC infrastructure team (not an autonomous agent). 20 active repos with strong code quality. 5 actionable PR opportunities across x402-api (3), sbtc-vault (1), and x402-sponsor-relay (1). All issues are self-identified via code audits. Strong alignment on Zest Protocol DeFi work. Already collaborating with us on MCP server maintenance.
